rulings.Case Title: YIN FALK VS. LINDA S CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Compelling Responses By Judgment Debtor Linda S Catron To Post-Judgment Discovery Request And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2012, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF YIN FALK'S Motion For Compelling Responses By Judgment Debtor Linda S. Catron To Post-Judgment Discovery Request And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel responses to post-judgment interrogatories, set one, and post-judgment demand for production of documents granted, no opposition filed. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,790. Moving party to submit proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TABITHA GLASER VS. JEWS FOR JESUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 15. DEFENDANT JEWS FOR JESUS, DEBORAH REMIGIO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There is a triable issue whether the release is enforceable. Specifically, there are triable issues whether the release is clear, unambiguous, and explicit and whether the release applies to plaintiff's claims because Plaintiff was the legal age of majority on the day that her mother executed the release. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT HASKINS VS. KAREN FRANCIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT THE HAROLD GORDON NELSON YEAR 2002 REVOCABLE TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The answer to the complaint does not bar the cross-complaint. The breach of contract cause of action is not uncertain. The cross-complaint is not necessarily time-barred. Cross-complainant's allegations regarding delayed discovery are adequately pled. Cross-complainant sufficiently alleged that cross-defendant breached his duty of care. Cross-defendant's motion to strike portions of the opposing points and authorities is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Verified suppemental or amended responses shall be delivered to counsel for the moving party within fifteen days of notice of this order. rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY WIENKE, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE VS. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, Na'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 18. Defendant JPmorgan Chase Bank, Na'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint. Sustained with fifteen days to amend as to the first through fifth causes of action, sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action, and overruled as to the seventh and eighth causes of action. Regarding the first cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead that Defendant unequivocally failed to contact her prior to recording the Notice of Default. Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead the legal effect of the contract and what portion of the contract Defendant breached, and that Plaintiff performed under the contract or was excused from performing. Regarding the third cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead a breach of the implied covenant arising out of a contract. Regarding the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing detrimental reliance. Regarding the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead fraud with particularity, including the "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means" of each alleged misrepresentation as well as names of the person/s who allegedly made the misrepresentation, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written. The sixth cause of action fails because Plaintiff has not stated a claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress under either a bystander or direct victim theory. The seventh and eighth causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 5. Plaintiff Deepmoney Llc'S Motion In Limine To Bar Evidence Of Irrelevant Previous Litigation. Denied without prejudice to re-filing the motion before the trial judge. At this pre-trial stage of the case, it is premature to seek to exclude particular purported evidence for all purposes. The proper remedy for plaintiff as to any effort to use the purported impermissible evidence on a pretrial motion is to object to the purported evidence. rulings.Case Title: HONGIK KIM VS. JOON ANG DAILY NEWS SF INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton And Special Motion To Strike Ptlf'S Complaint [Ccp 425.16]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT JOON ANG DAILY NEWS SF INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion And Special Motion To Strike Ptlf'S Complaint [Ccp 425.16]. Granted. Defendant satisfied with first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute because the alleged defamation was made in a public forum and concerned an issue of public interest. Plaintiff fails to present evidence showing the required minimal merit of his defamation claims. Plaintiff is a limited public figure and he does not shown that defendant acted with actual malice by clear and convincing evidence. Plaintiff does not show that defendant knew that the statements were false or acted with reckless disregard as to whether they were true. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NOEL WEEKS VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT LINDA CATRON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Defendant shall pay $500 to plaintiff by July 25, 2012 to cover the cost of obtaining the default. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BOB KORMAN et al VS. NICOLE SCHOTT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. Defendant satisfied the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute by showing that her review is protected speech because it was made in a public forum and addresses issues of public concern. Plaintiffs failed to show the required minimum merit to either of their claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
